doc: Pure-TS port of MPFR's mpfr_number_p: 'ordinary number' (finite) predicate. Returns true iff x is finite — kind 'normal' or 'zero' — and false for ±Inf or NaN. Includes signed zero. The C reference defers to the MPFR_IS_FP macro (`!IS_NAN && !IS_INF`); we express it positively. Class 'arithmetic' (50ms budget).
divergence_from_c: C returns int; TS returns boolean. Never throws. Note that NaN reaching this predicate is fully expected and produces false — this is the only one of the predicate family whose answer is 'NaN → false' from an intent perspective (the others happen to return false on NaN as a consequence of the kind dispatch).
refs: mpfr/src/isnum.c L24–L28 — the C reference (one-line body returning MPFR_IS_FP(x)).
mpfr/src/mpfr-impl.h §MPFR_IS_FP — the macro definition (NOT NaN AND NOT Inf).
src/core.ts L300–L302 — isFinite helper has identical semantics.
